It only checks files with `.py`, `.sh`, `.bash`, `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.toml` extensions (for `#` comments) or `.js`, `.ts`, etc. (for `//` comments)
It counts CONSECUTIVE comment lines that don't start with annotation prefixes (`# rationale:`, `# silent-ok:`, `# TODO:`, `# FIXME:`, etc.)
If the consecutive count reaches the threshold (`COMMENT_BLOAT_WARN`, default 3), it blocks with `BLOCK:<count>`
If any individual comment line exceeds `COMMENT_BLOAT_LONG_LINE` (default 90 chars), it blocks with `LONG:<len>`
